compiled_state.title: Write failing integration tests for Jira bridge outbound LINK/UNLINK sync
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Write failing (RED) integration tests that define the expected behavior for LINK and UNLINK event processing in bridge-outbound.py before any implementation begins.
**Why**: TDD discipline requires tests that document expected behavior before code is written. These tests serve as the executable specification for all subsequent implementation stories.
**Scope**:
- IN: RED tests for LINK event processing (relates_to mapped to Jira Relates link type), UNLINK event processing (read-before-delete with both-endpoint matching), pre-create dedup check, graceful degradation on Jira rejection, name-based link type lookup caching, 404-as-already-gone handling
- OUT: Implementation code, round-trip tests (those belong to the verification story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, test files exist that exercise LINK event processing through process_outbound and all tests fail (RED) because the implementation does not yet exist
  <- Satisfies: "bridge-outbound.py processes LINK events"
- When this story is complete, test files exist that exercise UNLINK event processing through process_outbound and all tests fail (RED)
  <- Satisfies: "UNLINK events remove the corresponding Jira link using a read-before-delete pattern"
- When this story is complete, test files exist that exercise idempotent dedup behavior and all tests fail (RED)
  <- Satisfies: "LINK event sync is idempotent: duplicate events for the same (source, target, relates_to) triple do not create duplicate Jira links"
- When this story is complete, test files exist that exercise graceful degradation on Jira API rejection and all tests fail (RED)
  <- Satisfies: "Jira rejection handled gracefully: writes bridge_alert and persist_relationship_rejection"
- Tests must be run and confirmed failing (RED) before any implementation story begins. The failing run result must be recorded in a story note.

## Considerations

- [Testing] All Jira API calls must use mock acli_client -- never hit real Jira endpoint
- [Testing] Follow existing test patterns in tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py
